---
title: "quiz10"
title-block-style: none
format: live-html
engine: knitr
sidebar:
  style: docked
resources:
  - study_hours_scores_A.csv
  - study_hours_scores_B.csv
---

{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_gradethis.qmd >}}

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  const showQ = params.get("q");

  // Show only the requested section
  if (showQ) {
    document.querySelectorAll("section[id]").forEach(section => {
      if (section.id !== showQ) section.style.display = "none";
    });
  }

  // Hide page title
  document.querySelectorAll(".quarto-title-block")
    .forEach(el => el.style.display = "none");

  // Hide ALL section titles (## ...)
  document.querySelectorAll("section[id] > h2")
    .forEach(h => h.style.display = "none");
});
</script>

## Q1 — Fit the Regression Model {#q1a}

Using the `study_hours_scores_A.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Return the estimated slope.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
coef(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
coef(model)
# 3.3124
```

## Q1 — Fit the Regression Model {#q1b}

Using the `study_hours_scores_B.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Return the estimated slope.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
coef(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
coef(model)["study_hours"]
# 2.9259
```

## Q2 — Compute R^2 {#q2a}

Using the `study_hours_scores_A.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Return the model's $R^2.$

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
___(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 0.9716
```


## Q2 — Compute R^2 {#q2b}

Using the `study_hours_scores_B.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Return the model's $R^2.$

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
___(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 0.9409
```


## Q3 — Compute the p-value for the slope {#q3a}

Using the `study_hours_scores_A.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Compute the p-value for testing $H_0: \beta_1 = 0.$

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
summary(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 3.133e-12
```

## Q3 — Compute the p-value for the slope {#q3b}

Using the `study_hours_scores_B.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Compute the p-value for testing $H_0: \beta_1 = 0.$

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
summary(model)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
summary(model)
# 2.997e-11
```

## Q4 — Predict the mean test score {#q4a}

Using the `study_hours_scores_A.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Predict the mean test score when `study_hours = 8`.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
predict(___, newdata = data.frame(study_hours = ___))
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_A.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
predict(model, newdata = data.frame(study_hours = 8))
# 69.2565
```


## Q4 — Predict the mean test score {#q4b}

Using the `study_hours_scores_B.csv` dataset, fit a simple linear regression model with `test_score` as the response and `study_hours` as the predictor.

Predict the mean test score when `study_hours = 8`.

```{webr}
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(___ ~ ___, data = quiz)
predict(___, newdata = data.frame(study_hours = ___))
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
quiz <- read.csv("study_hours_scores_B.csv")

model <- lm(test_score ~ study_hours, data = quiz)
predict(model, newdata = data.frame(study_hours = 8))
# 69.6408
```
